page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs based on the ARMv9 arm64 architecture, specifically utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks place the c8gn.xlarge in the average tier for most operations. It achieves a single-core stress-ng score of 1707.39 and a multi-core score of 6817.26. Memory bandwidth is measured at 462.19 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 96.29 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The instance demonstrates strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression performance at 14.43 GB/sec. However, it shows weaker performance in Passmark CPU tests and poor encryption performance due to the lack of certain extended instruction sets, though standard AES cryptography is supported.

The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps, making it optimized for network and EBS workloads. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based architecture offers a resource-dense compute profile with dedicated physical cores, avoiding hyperthreading overhead. It is well-suited for workloads such as static web serving, database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose compute tasks that benefit from dedicated ARM cores and high network throughput, while being less optimal for heavy cryptographic or large-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs powered by the 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 8.0 GB of memory and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate average performance across most compute and memory tasks, highlighted by strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 14.43 GB/sec and a static web serving throughput of 9.85 GB/sec. However, it exhibits weaker performance in cryptographic and LLM inference tests. This instance offers a cost-efficient, resource-dense profile for dedicated ARM workloads, making it highly suitable for static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and network-optimized applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated ARMv9 cores, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth, ideal for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.xlarge is an ARM-based compute-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM-powered, network-optimized compute instance featuring dedicated physical cores and balanced memory bandwidth for efficient web serving and database operations.
bullet_points: Powered by 4 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 cores at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
Delivers 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression performance of 14.43 GB/sec
Best suited for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
